title: 1.1: Tests that add_header() with subject_prefix parameter modifies Subject header to '[PREFIX] Original' format when message contains existing Subject value
1.2: Tests that add_header() sets the Subject header to only the prefix value when a subject_prefix parameter is provided to a message with no existing Subject header
1.3: Tests that add_header() injects only text headers when processing plain text email messages
1.4: Tests that add_header() injects only HTML headers wrapped in table tags when processing HTML email messages
1.5: Tests that add_header() injects text headers into plain text parts and HTML headers into HTML parts when processing multipart/alternative messages
1.6: Tests that add_header() injects headers into valid message parts while handling invalid string parts gracefully in multipart messages
1.7: Tests that add_header() processes the add_header_multipart.eml fixture file and returns non-empty output for structurally inconsistent multipart messages
2.1: Places the test for adding subject prefix to messages with existing Subject header in tests/test_email_utils.py
2.2: Places the test for adding subject prefix to messages without Subject header in tests/test_email_utils.py
2.3: Places the test for injecting headers into plain text email messages in tests/test_email_utils.py
2.4: Places the test for injecting headers into HTML email messages in tests/test_email_utils.py
2.5: Places the test for injecting headers into multipart/alternative email messages in tests/test_email_utils.py
2.6: Places the test for handling multipart messages with invalid parts in tests/test_email_utils.py
2.7: Places the test for processing the add_header_multipart.eml fixture file in tests/test_email_utils.py
3.1: Uses pytest framework with standard assert statements across all tests
3.2: Follows the test naming pattern test_[function]_[scenario] for all test functions
4.1: Uses load_eml_file() helper for loading test fixture files when testing with pre-existing malformed message files
